goal: Port structural.complexity.npath → .combinatorial, lift NPath vocabulary onto Language ABC, fix Java switch_expression bug

summary.title: Port structural.complexity.npath → .combinatorial (view-native + Java switch_expression fix)
summary.what: Renamed structural.complexity.npath → structural.complexity.combinatorial. Lifted 13 NPath grammar vocabulary methods onto Language ABC + filled all 11 grammars. Added Block StrEnum + missing Conditional/Loop members. Ported the Nejmeh-1988 recurrence onto Structure.combinatorial(c). Built run_combinatorial; retired legacy_v2_shim wrapper + the 850-line _structural/npath.py kernel. Fixed the documented Java switch_expression under-count and a same-shape JS/TS switch_body wrapper bug found while porting. 494 tests pass (+12 new combinatorial regression tests; Python parity check against the legacy kernel: 1307 functions, zero diffs).
summary.why: Continuing the v2 view-native cutover. NPath was the last shim-wrapped rule in the complexity family. The rename clarifies what NPath distinguishes from cyclomatic (multiplicative path counting, not just decision-point counting). The Java bug was known but unfixed in v1.0.1; doing it during this port avoided a second pass through the same code. The JS/TS switch_body bug was caught by a regression test written from the recurrence definition rather than from prior behaviour.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.complexity.npath → .combinatorial, lift NPath vocabulary onto Language ABC, fix Java switch_expression bug

decisions.chose: Lift onto Language ABC now (vs inline + later lift) — closes int_32af95fb scope.
Rename threshold param npath_threshold → combinatorial_threshold; _compat.py handles migration.
Java switch_nodes returns a frozenset (not single str) so {SWITCH_STATEMENT, SWITCH_EXPRESSION} can coexist; same for case_nodes with SWITCH_LABEL + SWITCH_RULE.
Added Block enum as a 10th category (statement-sequence wrappers — distinct from Wrapper, which wraps names/definitions).
Kept feature parity with legacy on Python (1307-fn zero-diff check) before touching the Java bug.
